feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Date format in Exporting Table to XLSX in SAC, from BW live data model
entry.content:
hi all,

we faced an issue with date format in Exporting Table to XLSX from SAC (from BW live data model).

we have dates in format dd.mm.yyyy (31.05.2024) and in excel it can't be chaged to Date format.

I tried changing the date formatting in Profile Settings as recommended in a similar question on this site (Date format in Exporting Table to XLSX in SAC)

But it didn't help, is it because of the Live Data Model? How can we change this?

Thank you.

entry.title: SAC - Using dynamic time calculations in Exception Aggregation is not supported.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am getting this error, "Using dynamic time calculations in Exception Aggregation is not supported". What I want is "Mix" Total and remove its formula dependency on the last total row calculation.

"Mix" is calculating from this formula, Below is the Screenshot of the formula.

I have used Restricted Measure for "PY_Price" and "PY Parent Price".

In the screenshot below, the calculation is logically correct: the "Vol Mix Diff" in the Totals row is zero, and ("PY_Price" - "PY Parent Price") is also zero, resulting in a "Mix" value of zero. However, I want the Grand Total of "Mix" to be derived from the sum of the above rows. Additionally, I need to calculate the "Mix Contribution" in another column, which would be computed as "Mix" divided by GrandTotal("Mix"). Unfortunately, "Add Calculation" -> "Aggregation" does not support this operation. Model is analytic.

Is there any way I can achieve that ?

entry.title: Export jobs to ACDOCP from profitability & Opex are duplicating the values inFP&A
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are using FP&A content and customized according to the customer needs. After planning we are exporting the plan data to ACDOCP from FI_IFP_IM_Profitability and FI_IFP_IM_OPEX models. When we load the OPEX load, we have observed that the measure values are getting offsetted and becomes zero. Opex load is causing this issue. Is there anything we are missing related to WBS Element and cost center.

Thanks

Sridhar

entry.title: Displaying Only Total Figures for Cost Elements in SAC Story
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a story in SAP Analytics Cloud where I have 1 to 9 series of cost elements. I want to show only the total figures for each series without displaying the details of each cost element. I do not want to use a chart; I just need to display the total numbers.

Can anyone guide me on how to do this?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Null values handling using LINK function in data actions in SAP SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm facing the issue with Null values while writing the logic in data actions. Here is the scenario. I have two Models. Model A, has units and sales as account. Model B, has additional sales and commission % as account. The data is written in Model B by reading the values from Model A and writing an additional calculation.

 Calculation:

IF Units = 0 or NULL then

Total Sales(model B) = Sales(model A) * Commission % (model B)

Else

Total Sales(model B) = Sales(model A) + Additional Sales(model B)

Please refer below screenshot.

I'm writing the same logic but no result is generated  for Sept and Oct as they have null values. This is what I'm writing this code in data action. I'm using the correct syntax for LINK,DATA and RESULTLOOKUP statement in the below code and this pseudo code is for reference.

IF LINK(Units(model A) > 0 then

DATA(Total Sales(model B)) = RESULTLOOKUP(Sales(model A)) + RESULTLOOKUP(Additional Sales(model B))

Else

DATA(Total Sales(model B)) = RESULTLOOKUP(Sales(model A) ) * RESULTLOOKUP(Commission % (model B))

 

Anyone who has faced this similar kind of issue or knows the workaround in this case. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: Getting Error in R Script
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am getting the below error while executing the R script in R Visualization. PFB of R script.

entry.title: Performance issues with standard GLAccountBalanceFlow CDS when loading SAC
entry.content:
Hi folks.

When using GLAccountBalanceFlow (OData service UI_FINS_GLAccountFlow) to load transactional data from S/4 into SAP Analytics Cloud, we are noticing that the performance is significantly worse than all other transactional CDS views we are using.

We are applying all recommended filters you would expect, such as Chart of Account, Controlling Area, Movement Type, Fiscal Period etc. and despite a relatively low data volume (<100k) the performance is still unacceptably bad. Attempting to run will hang for several hours and then raise short dumps due to memory outage.

If this is the CDS view that is intended as the SAP standard solution for loading closing Balances, something is going wrong. Are there any recommended steps or prerequisites to get this to work better? It is only this one CDS that seems to be having such issues.

Kind Regards

entry.title: SAC Forecast chart feature seems missing
entry.content:
Hello community.

I have a question about finding the forecast add-in in a time series chart. With the previous version of SAC stories I could locate it in the "3 dot" menu of the chart, in the "Add" section.

But in the new optimized version I wasn't able to find it anywhere. Did anyone from the community fased a similar issue? How can I solve it? 

Thank you in advance 

 

#SAP #SAC#Forecast

entry.title: Waterfall Chart: Color Subtotals like Increase / Decrease
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

in Waterfall Chart in SAC we want to color a subtotal bar according to its value. We can switch between 3 and 4 colors in waterfall color layout. If we select three colors, subtotal is colored like a total. When we change to 4 colors, we can define a certain color for subtotals. But we want the subtotal bar to change its color according to the value.

In the following screenshot the second bar should be formatted like the following bars. (green or red according to its value)

 

Is this possible somehow? Am I missing something here. Datasource is BW4HANA live connection. Subtotal is a hierarchy node in BW.

An alternative could be, to not show the subtotal bar at all, but only the child nodes. But I have not found a way to do this either, so far.

Any help is much appreciated. Thanks.

Dominik

entry.title: Forecast prediction periods - SAC
entry.content:
Hello, 
I'm struggling with prediction both in classic and in optimized story:
Classic story: 
I've created a report with actual from January to October - and I want to create a 12 months predictive forecast.
But for some reason both my chart and table, let me forecast only one month
and I can't find where to change this default.
how can it be changed?

Optimized story: 

I can't find how to add the predictive forecast at all, it's not in the menu nor in the chart options:
How does it work?

 

 

 

thank you

entry.title: getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers(); seems to be slow?
entry.content:
I have made a script which allows me to translate filters from one model used in one story, to a different model running a different story.

I start by getting the active selected members in the 4 present filters.

var activeSelectedMembers = InputControl_16.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();
var activeSelectedMembersProject = InputControl_14.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();
var activeSelectedMembersTime = InputControl_12.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();
var activeSelectedMembersGroup = InputControl_4.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();

After getting these I am performing quite a bit of logic to translate the origin model filters to fit the target model's filters. In the end I construct a NavigationUtils to open the other story and transferring correct filtering. Everything works perfectly, although seems to be very slow.
Through some performance tracking I have narrowed it down to the first 4 lines of "getInputControlDataSource()". These 4 together takes up 95% of the processing time.

In total the button to construct the fancy link, takes around 20sec to complete. Which is enough time that I had to perform some button logic to tell the user that something was actually happening.

Curious why these are so heavy on performance?
And if there might be a better way of getting active members. Maybe a method of getting all 4 at the same time? Sequential queries is possibly slower?

entry.title: Carry Forward - deletes the first opening month - SAC
entry.content:
Hey 
I have a very simple carry forward that doesn't work.
I use a flow dimension with - Opening, changes and closing. 
The opening balance is already copied from last actual to January 2025
Then changes planned are manually inserted on 2025 (changes are named "plug number" in my case). 
My goal is to calculate the closing. 
It looks like I did something wrong in my formula (below) because the Jan2025 opening is deleted, and the carryforward doesn't take it in account.
This is the code, and below, print screens

MEMBERSET[d/Date]=BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/YM] ,%P_03_BUD_YEAR% )
MEMBERSET[d/Measures]="LAST_VALUE"
DATA()= 
CARRYFORWARD([d/IL_D_INS_FLOW],"OPENING","ENDING","OPENING"+"PLUG")

thank you 🙂

entry.title: Valuing dimensions with members # using already available rows
entry.content:
Dear experts,
I would need some help with the development of a Data Action.
After a first step of Cross-Model Copy the row situation is as follows:

ViewEntityMarketProductsCustomerChannelSales CategoryIntercompanyDistr. FlowNet Sales (Local)Std Cost EoL (Local)Std Cost Mark Up Reverse (Local)
#V10VNProd1DEALERRETCOMMON#STOCK10000––
#V10#Prod1#####–5000

I have taken 2 rows as an example, the purpose would be to go and “merge” the two rows. So with end result the following:

ViewEntityMarketProductsCustomerChannelSales CategoryIntercompanyDistr. FlowNet Sales (Local)Std Cost EoL (Local)Std Cost Mark Up Reverse (Local)
#V10VNProd1DEALERRETCOMMON#STOCK100005000

Do you have any advice on how this can be done?

Thanks for the help

entry.title: SAC Excel MEMERPROPERTY not returning Description
entry.content:
I have a number of Excel files setup using the SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY function to label rows and columns.
Today I am finding that previously working cells now give me a #VALUE! error in Excel with the SAC Excel Add-in.

For example:
=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY(DS_1, "Measures", "qty_u", "Description")
This used to return the Description of my measure "Unit Qty" - today I get #VALUE!
=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY(DS_1, "Measures", "qty_u", "Name")
Still returns "qty_u" as it previously did

=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY(DS_1, "Version", "public.2024FC", "firstPlanDate")
works and returns my custom property as defined in the dimension tables in the modeler

=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY(DS_1, "Version", "public.2024FC", "Description")
used to return "2024FC" but now results in a #VALUE! error in excel

 

Has something changed? SAC update? Add-in Update?

What am I missing here - being able to pull in the Description from my Account dimensions, measures, versions, etc. was a huge time savings in some report setups I have been using for several months.

Thanks

entry.title: SAC story page filter default selection with Button Script
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a scenario for one our story where we would like to filter only on Version with Property ( Start_Period) which has date values in it.

Example: Version Dimension

 

 

 

We have story which we need to display with the only column with:

 

 

We are trying to fix story filter with Date and Version Property (Start_Period) and getting an error while running the button with the below script.

var selections = Table_1.getDataSource().getDataSelections();
var member = PlanningModel_1.getMember("Version",selections[0]["Version"]);
var prop = member.properties;
var planning = Table_1.getPlanning();
var SM1 = prop["Start_Period"];
var dateparam = "[Date].[FYP].&[" +SM1+ "]";

 

//Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Date","[Date].[FYP].&[202401]");
Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Date",dateparam);

 

There are no errors in script, however when we run Button it throws an error as below:

 

Please advise.

Regards,

Ram

entry.title: SAC - Signflip in Calculation of Previous Years Values
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

we have a problem in SAP Analytics Cloud, data modeling with the Signflip of EXP-Accounts. 

Situation

We have an Account-Dimension with a Sign-Flip-Property, which should enable different signs for the EXP-Accounts (Sign Flip for Base Amount shows the Costs as a positive value / Sign Flip for Management shows Costs as a negative Amount). 

This works well, as decribed in the Documentation (SAP Help Portal). Also for the Calculation (Row 3 - Calculation of "Direct Margin = Revenue - Direct Cost of Sales") it works fine, after setting the right Priorities for the Calculation. Exmaple see Screenshot below. 

Problem

We need to calculate the Previous Year Values. But here the Signflip "destroys" the calculation when it comes to the time navigation. 

The first 2 Columns (green) are correct ans also the result (Direct Margin) is calculated correctly.

  • LC (no Signflip) shows always positive Values
  • LC shows Cost as negative Values

In the last Column (red) we can see the problem for the previous Year Calculation:

  • LC - (PY) does show the Cost as negative value, but the calculation of Direct Margin is wrong (it adds the 2 Accounts above). I would expect 2,960k - 2,178k = 782k as a direct margin. Like in the middle column for LC. 

Background

New Model Type, optimized Story. 

LC (no Signflip) - is the Measure in the Import-Model. 

LC - is a calculated Measure to reflect the signflip:

 

LC - (PY) - is a calculated measure to get the previous year value from LC (only Actuals):

 

It would be great if someone can help here! 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAC / Optimized Story/ Hide Parent total values in sac
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Is there a way to hide the parent totals in a table? Only the child values are relevant for this tabble but it is aggregating the values

Many thanks for your hints!

Attila

entry.title: Need Blob to text conversion in Data Sphere
entry.content:
Hi folks,

I have table in S/4 hana STXl to store the long text for all other tables which i am using it in Data sphere to get text but i am failing to convert the clustd value  TO_CHAR(TO_NCLOB(TO_BINARY('FF06020102028000343130330000000063010000121F9D0232663E060606461E20C1C0CA80058430F8307832F831B832AC85A85C05166601538C1CEB2082FBA0', 'HEX'))) text but it was giving an error for conversion.

 

 

Could anyone please help me here to convert this in data sphere or any suggestion would be helpful.

entry.title: Data Access Control in Modeler in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi all,


Is it possible to have Data Access Control for data from model on SAC with same approach as we have in Datasphere? Model on SAC is with imported data not via live connection.


I have concept of authorization based on dimensions with so many values and there is so many users. For example, each Project Manager should see data only for one or more Projects, but dimension Project has over 5000 values and it's not possible to maintain it manually like on the screenshot below.

And this is not only issue, but many Users have more than one dimension that his authorizations are based on. Is it possible to implement this concept of data access control in SAC? Below is preview of authorization concept from Datasphere.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Is it Possible in SAC to split a line chart into multiple charts based on a dimension?
entry.content:
So using Sales as a measure with Time as the X axis dimension trend.

Now want to see this trend split into multiple charts based on an additional dimension, "Product" for example
